The ₦50M breach that could have been prevented for ₦200k"  A Nigerian fintech lost ₦50M to a cyberattack last year.  The vulnerability that caused it would have cost ₦200k to fix.  Here's what happened 🧵

The first cyberattack in history using prompt injection. Attackers used Meta’s chatbot as a tool to take over Instagram accounts belonging to well-known people, brands, and institutions. By manipulating Meta’s AI support system, they convinced it to perform a critical administrative operation: changing or adding an email address associated with the victim’s account.  Basic mistake: using LLM as a security boundary.  The attacker contacted Meta’s bot, provided the username of the account they wanted to take over, and asked it to link that account to a new email address controlled by the attacker. In practice, this meant that the person controlling the new email address could receive or provide the confirmation code, and then use the modified recovery channel to reset the password and take over the account.  AI support became a path for bypassing account security. If a chatbot can change an email address or initiate account recovery without independent verification of the owner, the attacker does not need to know the password or break through traditional security controls. It is sufficient to convince the automated support operator to perform an operation that the attacker should not normally be allowed to request.  theverge.com/tech/941179/met…

Cyberattack on humanitarian organization World Food Program exposes sensitive data of vulnerable population. Affected 600,000 households in Gaza, names, ID numbers, phone numbers, location data, all exfiltrated. The timing is specific. Israel's Supreme Court had just upheld a requirement forcing aid organizations to hand over workers' personal data as a condition of operating in Gaza. In 2022 it was the Red Cross (515,000 people). In 2023, the Norwegian Refugee Council. This time it's WFP. The sector has had a poor track record. thenewhumanitarian.org/news/…

Breaking: H.E Atiku Abubakar has called for a comprehensive and independent investigation into the leakage of confidential data from the INEC voter registration database.  INEC's explanation that there was no external cyberattack, but that the data was accessed using valid official credentials, raises serious concerns about possible internal security failures or political interference.   He urged the commission to fully disclose how the data was obtained, who accessed it, who received it, and how it was released.  Atiku warned that the incident could undermine public confidence in the electoral process and threaten the credibility of the 2027 general elections if it is not thoroughly investigated and transparently addressed.

Key detection signals from Redis themselves: watch for unexplained server crashes originating from the Lua engine, anomalous network egress from your Redis instance, and unknown command execution by the redis-server user. If you haven't patched, treat those as active compromise indicators. 👇 thehackernews.com/2026/06/au…

【PAN-OS GlobalProtectの認証バイパス脆弱性、悪用試行を確認】  Palo Alto Networksは、PAN-OSのGlobalProtectに関するCVE-2026-0257を公表し、認証バイパスにより不正なVPN接続を確立される可能性を示しました。ベンダーは限定的な悪用試行を認識しており、Exploit MaturityもATTACKEDとされています。  対象はGlobalProtectポータルまたはゲートウェイで、特定の認証オーバーライドCookieと証明書設定が関係します。VPNは侵害されると内部ネットワークへの初期アクセスになりやすく、影響は単一機器に留まりません。  日本の組織は、対象バージョン、設定条件、修正版適用状況を即時確認するべきです。更新が難しい場合は、専用証明書の利用、認証オーバーライド無効化、接続元制限、ログ監視強化を検討してください。  #PANOS #GlobalProtect #CVE20260257 #VPN #脆弱性管理 #SOC #日本 security.paloaltonetworks.co…

【Android 2026年6月更新、CVE-2025-48595に標的型悪用の可能性】  Androidの2026年6月セキュリティ情報では、複数のCriticalを含む多数の脆弱性が修正されています。GoogleはCVE-2025-48595について、限定的・標的型の悪用を受けている可能性があるとしています。  Android端末は個人利用だけでなく、営業、物流、店舗、医療、自治体の現場端末としても広く使われています。未更新端末が業務アプリ、VPN、メール、SaaSに接続している場合、端末侵害から認証情報窃取へつながるリスクがあります。  MDMではパッチレベル2026-06-01/2026-06-05への到達状況を確認し、更新できない端末は業務アクセスを制限する判断が必要です。日本の組織では、Android更新をPCパッチと同じ運用サイクルに組み込むべきです。  #Android #CVE #モバイルセキュリティ #MDM #脆弱性管理 #SOC #日本 source.android.com/docs/secu…

Interlock ransomware group pivots from user-driven attacks to zero-day exploitation, deploying AI-generated Slopoly backdoor to bypass security controls. Active campaign exploited Cisco FMC vulnerability for 36 days before patching.  Key technical details: • CVE-2026-20131: Zero-day in Cisco Secure FMC enabling root RCE via crafted HTTP requests with serialized Java • Slopoly backdoor: AI-generated PowerShell C2 framework with WebSocket persistence and real-time communication • Hotta Killer: Custom utility exploiting CVE-2025-61155 in GameDriverX64.sys for kernel-level EDR disabling (T1685) • Memory-resident Java webshells intercept HTTP requests, decrypt commands, execute in-memory to evade AV • LOLBAS abuse: BITSAdmin, PowerShell, AZCopy for staging, lateral movement, and Azure Blob exfiltration (T1567.002)  Attack chain methodology: • Phase 1: Shifted from drive-by downloads to direct infrastructure targeting via network edge vulnerabilities • Phase 2-3: Volatility for credential extraction, Certipy for AD CS privilege escalation, NetSupport RAT deployment • Phase 4-5: Advanced Port Scanner reconnaissance, RDP pivoting to DCs/Exchange, HAProxy nodes for exfiltration masking • Phase 6: PsExec domain-wide ransomware deployment with .interlock extension and !__README__!.txt notes  Hunt for AZCopy activity to unfamiliar Azure destinations, NetSupport/AnyDesk from servers, and recurring /api/commands HTTP beaconing patterns.  #DFIR_Radar
Redisで2年物の脆弱性CVE-2026-23479が修正。任意OSコマンド実行。自律型AIツールが発見したもの。CVSSv3スコア8.8、v4スコア7.7。要認証たがユーザが既定で保持する権限で悪用可能。 thehackernews.com/2026/06/au…

【Redisの認証済みRCE脆弱性 CVE-2026-23479】  Redisで、認証済みユーザーが任意OSコマンド実行につなげ得るCVE-2026-23479が修正されました。  この脆弱性はUse-After-Freeに関係し、Redis 7.2.0以降の一部バージョンに影響します。  認証が必要な脆弱性ではありますが、Redisがインターネットに露出している、デフォルトユーザー権限が強い、アプリケーション用アカウントに過剰なACLがある場合は影響が大きくなります。  SOCでは、Redisのバージョン、外部公開有無、`CONFIG`や`EVAL`の実行履歴、Redisホストからの不審なプロセス起動や外部通信を確認してください。  #Redis #CVE #RCE #脆弱性管理 #クラウドセキュリティ #SOC thehackernews.com/2026/06/au…

【PAN-OS GlobalProtectの認証バイパス悪用をRapid7が観測】  PAN-OS GlobalProtectのCVE-2026-0257について、Rapid7が実環境での悪用を観測しています。  この問題は、特定条件下で未認証の攻撃者がVPN接続を確立できる可能性があるもので、境界機器を初期侵入口にされる点が非常に重要です。  VPNは一度突破されると、内部ネットワークへの到達性、認証情報探索、横展開の足場になりやすいため、CVSSだけでなく実悪用と配置場所を基準に優先度を上げるべきです。  SOCでは、GlobalProtectの接続ログ、不審なCookie認証、ローカル管理アカウント利用、接続後の内部スキャンや認証試行を確認してください。  #PaloAltoNetworks #PANOS #GlobalProtect #VPN #CISA #KEV #SOC rapid7.com/blog/post/etr-rap…

【Oracle WebLogicのCVE-2024-21182がKEV入り】  Oracle WebLogic ServerのCVE-2024-21182が、実悪用を受けてCISA KEVに追加されました。  この脆弱性は、未認証の攻撃者がネットワーク経由でT3またはIIOPに到達できる場合に悪用される可能性があり、サーバーデータへの不正アクセスにつながります。  CVSSだけを見ると最上位ではありませんが、未認証・ネットワーク到達可能・アプリケーションサーバーという条件が揃うため、実運用上の優先度は高いです。  防御側は、WebLogicのパッチ適用、T3/IIOPの公開範囲、WAFやFWの制御、サーバーログ上の不審な接続を確認してください。  #Oracle #WebLogic #CISA #KEV #CVE #脆弱性管理 #SOC thehackernews.com/2026/06/or…

【Microsoft Defenderの脆弱性2件が実悪用】  Microsoft DefenderのCVE-2026-41091とCVE-2026-45498について、実悪用が確認されています。  CVE-2026-41091は権限昇格によりSYSTEM権限取得につながる可能性があり、CVE-2026-45498はDefenderの機能停止を引き起こす恐れがあります。  重要なのは、これは初期侵入そのものよりも、侵入後の権限拡大や防御回避に使われやすい点です。  SOCでは、DefenderのMalware Protection EngineとAntimalware Platformのバージョン、保護機能の停止イベント、直前の不審プロセス実行を確認する必要があります。  #MicrosoftDefender #CISA #KEV #EDR #WindowsSecurity #CVE #SOC helpnetsecurity.com/2026/05/…

【AndroidとLinuxの実悪用CVEにCISAが警告】  CISAが、Android FrameworkのCVE-2025-48595とLinux kernelのCVE-2022-0492について、実悪用を踏まえた警告を出しています。  Android側は権限昇格、Linux側はcgroups v1を介した権限昇格やコンテナ脱出につながる可能性があり、端末管理とサーバー基盤の両方で優先度が高い内容です。  特に、モバイル端末、コンテナ基盤、特権コンテナ、古いLinuxカーネルが混在する環境では、単なるパッチ適用状況だけでなく、権限昇格後の挙動も監視対象になります。  SOCでは、Androidの2026年6月パッチレベル、cgroups v1の利用、コンテナからホスト側への異常アクセス、EDR上の権限昇格イベントを確認したいところです。  #CISA #KEV #Android #Linux #CVE #コンテナセキュリティ #脆弱性対応 bleepingcomputer.com/news/se…

sample_tweets.text: 📋 Daily Intel 6/4｜AIは国家戦略とサイバー防衛へ、ロボットと宇宙は現場で静かに進む  🎯 今日の主役  6月4日朝の主役は、AIが「便利な個人ツール」から、国家戦略・サイバー防衛・職場の自動化までを横断する基盤になっていることです。  ホワイトハウスは6月2日に、先進AIのイノベーションと安全保障に関する大統領令を出しました。柱は、政府システムと重要インフラのサイバー防衛、AI-enabled defensive tools、任意のフロンティアモデル評価・早期アクセス枠です。AnthropicとSam Altmanも、米国のAI主導権と安全性の両立に反応しました。  同じ日にAnthropicは、悪用アカウントの行動をMITRE ATT&CKへマッピングしたサイバー脅威分析も公開しています。宇宙ではSpaceXがStarlink衛星打ち上げを進め、Rocket LabはNeutronのエンジン適合テストを共有しました。ロボットではBoston Dynamicsが物流現場のStretchとAI活用を扱うウェビナーを案内しています。  問いはシンプルです。自分の仕事や家庭の中で、AIに任せる判断と、人間が最後に持つ判断をどこで分けるべきでしょうか。  📎 直近24時間の動き  - ホワイトハウスが先進AIのイノベーションと安全保障に関する大統領令を公表。   whitehouse.gov/presidential-… - AnthropicがAI大統領令について、米国のAIリーダーシップに関わる重要な一歩と反応。   nitter.privacyredirect.com/AnthropicAI/status/206… - Sam Altmanが、AIで米国が主導することと安全性の両立を支持する反応を投稿。   nitter.privacyredirect.com/sama/status/2061973280… - Anthropicが、AIを使ったサイバー脅威をMITRE ATT&CKへマッピングした分析を公開。   anthropic.com/news/AI-enable… - SpaceXがカリフォルニアから24基のStarlink衛星打ち上げを案内。   nitter.privacyredirect.com/SpaceX/status/20621952… - Rocket LabがNeutronのエンジン適合テストを共有。   nitter.privacyredirect.com/RocketLab/status/20619… - Boston Dynamicsが、物流ロボットStretchとAI活用に関するウェビナーを案内。   bosdyn.co/4u9HYMZ - AIモデルの計算量拡張をめぐるコミュニティ議論も継続。   nitter.privacyredirect.com/scaling01/status/20619…  🧭 今日の焦点 3件  1. AI政策は生活者のツール選びにも影響する  AI大統領令は一見すると国家レベルのニュースですが、実際には私たちが使うAIツールの安全基準、企業導入、教育、セキュリティ、政府システムの運用ルールに影響していきます。  家庭や小さなチームでも、AIを「速くする道具」としてだけでなく、「何を任せてよいかを決める仕組み」として扱う必要が出てきます。  2. サイバー防衛はAI時代の生活インフラ  Anthropicの分析は、AIが悪用される現実を、抽象論ではなく攻撃パターンとして整理しています。これは企業のSOCだけでなく、個人のアカウント管理、家族のフィッシング対策、仕事用ツールの権限設計にもつながる話です。  自分の生活でまず見るべきなのは、パスワード、二要素認証、共有リンク、AIツールに入れている機密情報です。  3. ロボットと宇宙は「見えない日常」を更新する  SpaceXのStarlink、Rocket LabのNeutron、Boston DynamicsのStretchは、どれも派手な未来像というより、通信・物流・配送・倉庫作業を少しずつ変える基盤です。  生活者にとって重要なのは、これらが遠い技術ではなく、通信品質、配送速度、在庫管理、災害時の接続性として戻ってくることです。  📊 数値スナップショット  - BTC: 66,677ドル（-6.39%） - ETH: 1,882ドル（-5.86%） - ADA: 0.2144ドル（-6.56%） - USD/JPY: 159.937（+0.37%）  暗号資産は弱く、円安方向の為替も家計・輸入コスト・海外サービス利用料の見え方に影響します。生活者目線では、資産価格だけでなく、AIサービス、通信、エネルギー、セキュリティのコストも見ておきたい局面です。  ⚡ 先行指標 Watch（48-72h）  - AI大統領令に続く企業・研究機関・政府機関の具体的な対応 - Anthropicのサイバー脅威分析に対するセキュリティ業界の反応 - AIツールの法人導入で、権限管理やログ保存がどう強調されるか - SpaceX/Starlinkの打ち上げ継続と通信インフラの拡張 - Rocket Lab Neutronの次の統合テスト - Stretchの倉庫・物流導入事例が、労働時間や作業設計にどう反映されるか  🔄 48-72h 分岐（観察軸）  - 強気分岐: AI政策が安全性と導入促進の両方を後押しし、サイバー防衛・物流自動化・宇宙通信の実装ニュースが続く。 - 中立分岐: 技術ニュースは多いが、生活への影響はまだ企業導入や政府方針の確認待ち。 - 弱気分岐: AI悪用やセキュリティ事故が注目され、個人・職場のAI利用ルール見直しが優先される。  一次ソース / 関連リンク  - White House: Promoting Advanced Artificial Intelligence Innovation and Security   whitehouse.gov/presidential-… - Anthropic: AI-enabled cyber threats and MITRE ATT&CK   anthropic.com/news/AI-enable… - Anthropic reaction to AI executive order   nitter.privacyredirect.com/AnthropicAI/status/206… - Sam Altman AI policy reaction   nitter.privacyredirect.com/sama/status/2061973280… - SpaceX launch update   nitter.privacyredirect.com/SpaceX/status/20621952… - Rocket Lab Neutron update   nitter.privacyredirect.com/RocketLab/status/20619… - Boston Dynamics Stretch   bosdyn.co/4u9HYMZ

ChatGPT要約  What We Learned Mapping a Year’s Worth of AI-Enabled Cyber Threats to MITRE ATT&CK  ・本記事要約日: 2026-06-04 ・本記事公開日: 2026-06-04  本質の要約  Anthropicは、2025年3月〜2026年3月の間に悪意あるサイバー活動で停止した832アカウントを分析し、その活動内容をMITRE ATT&CKフレームワークへマッピングした。  調査の目的は、「実際の攻撃者はAIをどのように利用しているのか」を定量的に把握することにある。  分析結果から判明したのは、AIはまだ単独で高度なサイバー攻撃を自律実行する段階には達していないものの、攻撃者の生産性向上には既に大きく寄与しているという事実である。  特に利用が多かったのは以下の領域だった。  * マルウェア開発支援 * スクリプト生成 * フィッシング文面作成 * 攻撃対象の調査・情報収集 * 脆弱性分析 * 攻撃手順の整理・自動化  一方で、高度な侵入後活動（Persistence、Privilege Escalation、Defense Evasionなど）は比較的少なく、現時点ではAIが攻撃の全工程を完全自動化している状況は確認されなかった。  しかし、攻撃チェーンの各工程にAIが浸透し始めており、今後はReconnaissanceからExfiltrationまでをエージェントが連続実行する方向へ進化する可能性が高い。  Anthropicは、AIによるサイバー脅威を理解するためにMITRE ATT&CKという共通言語で観測結果を整理することが重要であり、防御側も同じフレームワークを活用して対策を強化すべきだと提言している。    ⸻  調査で分かった主なポイント  1. AIは攻撃者の「効率化ツール」になっている  現状のAI利用は、  * コード作成 * スクリプト修正 * 自動化支援 * 情報整理  が中心である。  従来は数時間〜数日かかっていた作業を数分〜数十分で実施できるようになっている。  2. AIが最も多く使われるのは攻撃前段階  特に以下の用途が目立つ。  * Reconnaissance（偵察） * Resource Development（攻撃準備） * Initial Access（侵入準備）  つまり「攻撃そのもの」よりも「攻撃準備工程」の効率化で大きな効果を発揮している。  3. 完全自律型攻撃はまだ限定的  世間では「AIハッカー」が話題になることが多いが、実際の観測結果では、  * 人間が計画 * AIが補助 * 人間が実行  という形態が大半だった。  4. 防御側も同じAIを活用できる  攻撃者だけでなく防御側も、  * 脆弱性分析 * ログ解析 * インシデント対応 * Threat Hunting  へAIを活用できる。  AnthropicはAIが攻撃・防御双方の能力を引き上げる「両刃の剣」であることを強調している。    ⸻  何が新しいのか  今回の新規性は、  「AIによるサイバー脅威」をMITRE ATT&CKフレームワークに体系的にマッピングし、実際の悪用事例を大規模に分析した点にある。  従来は、  * AIは危険か * AIは攻撃を強化するか  という議論が中心だった。  本レポートでは、  * どの攻撃工程で * どの程度 * どのように  AIが使われているのかを実データで示した。  ⸻  何ができるようになったのか  この分析により、  * AI悪用の傾向をMITRE ATT&CKで分類可能 * 攻撃チェーンごとのAI利用状況を可視化可能 * 防御優先度の判断が容易 * AI活用型攻撃の早期検知ルール設計が可能  になった。  また、防御側組織は自社SOCやCSIRTの検知ルールをMITRE ATT&CKベースで再評価し、AI時代向けに更新しやすくなった。  ⸻  どのプランで使えるのか  本記事は新しいAIモデルの発表ではないため該当なし。  ⸻  この記事で1番言いたいことを一言で  「AIはまだ攻撃者の代わりではない。しかし、攻撃者の生産性をすでに劇的に高めている。」  ⸻  単語帳  用語説明 MITRE ATT&CKサイバー攻撃者の戦術・技術・手順を体系化した業界標準フレームワーク Reconnaissance攻撃対象の調査・偵察活動 Resource Development攻撃に必要なインフラやツールの準備 Initial Access最初の侵入経路を確立する工程 Persistence侵入後に継続的アクセスを維持する技術 Privilege Escalation権限昇格 Defense Evasionセキュリティ製品や監視を回避する技術 Exfiltration情報の持ち出し Threat Hunting攻撃痕跡を能動的に探索する活動 SOCSecurity Operations Center CSIRTComputer Security Incident Response Team Malware悪意あるソフトウェア Phishing偽メールや偽サイトによる認証情報窃取手法 Threat Intelligence脅威情報の収集・分析活動 ATT&CK Mapping攻撃行動をMITRE ATT&CKへ分類する作業  引用  anthropic.com/news/AI-enable…

Anthropicが、サイバー攻撃でのAIの悪用を1年分にわたって分析し、「攻撃者の危険度は技術力の高さや使う手法の数だけでは測りにくくなった」とする報告を公表しました。Claudeの不正利用で凍結した832アカウントを、攻撃手法の分類体系であるMITREのATT&CKにマッピングしたもので、中リスク以上とされる攻撃者の割合は調査の前半から後半で33%から56%へ約1.7倍に増えています。  AIが高度な作業まで肩代わりするため手法の数や技術力だけでは危険度を見極めにくく、分かれ目はAIに攻撃の各段階を順につなげて自律実行させる周辺の作り込み（scaffolding）にあるとしています。  Anthropicは、危険度の採点を目的に、AIがどれだけ攻撃を後押ししたかを測る独自の指標「ARiES」を新設。さらに、自律的に攻撃を組み立てる動きを捉えられるようATT&CK自体の拡張をMITREと協議しているとしています。  手口の数や技術力で攻撃者を格付けする発想がAIエージェントの時代には通用しなくなりつつあることを1年分の実データで裏づけた報告になっています。  【要点の整理】  ・AIの用途で最も多いのはマルウェア開発で、832アカウント中560が利用。難読化やローカル端末からのデータ収集、防御の無効化が続き、AIは主に攻撃の準備段階で使われている  ・侵入後に内部を渡り歩く横展開にAIを使う例は少数だが、横展開を任せた54アカウントの平均リスクは56.4と全体平均46.8を約10ポイント上回り、高リスクを示す最も強い指標  ・攻撃者の技術力、使う手法の数（中央値16）、利用インターフェースは、いずれも危険度との相関が弱く、80%がエージェント型のコーディングツールClaude Codeを使うなど、従来の見分け方が通用しにくくなっている  ・Anthropicが新設したリスク指標ARiESは、脅威・脆弱性・影響の3軸を掛け算でなく足し算で合成する設計。1つの要素がゼロでも全体がゼロにならず、被害が未確認でもAIが攻撃能力を与えた事実をスコアに残せる  ・最大スコア100を記録した2025年11月公表の国家関与とされるスパイ活動（GTG-1002）も、使った手法の数だけ見れば中リスク級。だが侵入テスト用のKali Linux上でClaude Codeを動かし、オープンソースの侵入テストツールをAIから直接呼べる形で統合して、偵察・脆弱性の悪用・認証情報の窃取・横展開までAIに自律実行させ、人間が指示したのは最終的なデータ持ち出しなど要所のみだったとされる  ・攻撃の各段階を自律的に統制する動きやリアルタイムの判断には現行のATT&CKに対応するIDが無く、AnthropicはMITREと枠組みの拡張を協議中  詳細は以下を参照： red.anthropic.com/2026/attac…

$CRWD $PANW $ZS $S $MSFT $GOOGL $AMZN solana:Pren1FvFX6J3E4kXhJuCiAD5aDmGEb7qJRncwA8Lkhw    Anthropic just published one of the clearest looks yet at how AI is changing cyberattacks.  The company analyzed 832 accounts banned for malicious cyber activity between March 2025 and March 2026.  These were Claude-related abuse cases where Anthropic had enough detail to map attacker behavior against MITRE ATT&CK.  The findings are important:  67.3% used AI to help write malware  6.5% used AI for lateral movement inside compromised networks  Medium-or-higher risk actors rose from 33% in the first six months to 56% in the second six months  AI-assisted phishing declined, while account discovery and post-compromise activity increased  That last point matters most.  Attackers are no longer using AI only to get into systems.  They are increasingly using AI after compromise:  discovering accounts moving laterally escalating privileges chaining attack steps making tactical decisions reducing human input  Anthropic’s key warning:  Traditional cyber frameworks are not fully capturing the danger of agentic AI attacks.  MITRE ATT&CK can map techniques, but it does not fully capture AI-enabled orchestration — where a model chains multiple attack stages together with minimal human control.  That changes the cybersecurity market.  The old question was:  How skilled is the attacker?  The new question is:  How much can the AI agent do for them?  Bull case for cybersecurity:  AI expands the threat surface and forces more spending on endpoint, identity, cloud, SOC automation, detection and AI-native defense.  But attackers may gain capability faster than legacy security frameworks can adapt.  AI is making weaker attackers more capable.  That is why cybersecurity may become one of the most durable AI spending categories.

This suggests that AI is helping attackers conduct increasingly sophisticated cyber operations with greater ease.  - The number of actors using AI for cyber operations is growing, and their actions carry higher risk  -Agentic scaffolding will make it possible for cyberattacks to be far more autonomous  - The MITRE ATT&CK framework doesn’t yet cover the autonomous actions that make these actors so dangerous.'

ランサムウェアに関与するとされる攻撃者が、役割を分けたAIエージェントのチームでEDR回避マルウェアの開発・検証ラボを運用していた事例が報告されています。AIネイティブな開発環境Cursor上で、中核の調整役をClaude-Opus-4.5のエージェントが担い、他のエージェントの規則を設定する構成だったとのこと。  「レッドチーム用」という名目はマルウェア開発に関するガードレールをすり抜けるためだったとみられ、実体は標的環境での隠密な侵入後活動のためのもので、既知のデータ窃取の活動とも結び付けられているとされます。  AIエージェントの活用は高度な攻撃の参入障壁を下げる一方、守る側のやるべきことは変わりません。多層防御を軸に、適時のパッチ適用や多要素認証、パスキー、有効なEDRの広範な展開といった基本の徹底が引き続き重要になります。  【要点の整理】  ・検知の発端は、Sophos X-Opsが顧客テナント内で捉えた異常な端末のアラート。同じ端末からは、ビーコン通信を正規のWeb通信に偽装するCobalt Strikeプロファイル、Telegramのbot API経由の外部C2、正規のWindows実行ファイルへ機能を保ったままシェルコードを注入するPythonスクリプト、背後のC2を隠すCloudflare Workerの中継などが確認され、スクリプトの多くはロシア語で書かれていたとのこと  ・攻撃者は検証ラボをLudus（仮想検証環境を手早く構築する基盤）で用意。Windows Server 2022のVMをSophos用・CrowdStrike用・EDRなしに分け、別途UbuntuにSliver（C2フレームワーク）のサーバーを併設するなど、正規の検証環境に近い構成。コードのIssueやコミットはMCP（AIを外部ツールと接続する標準規格）経由でGitへ連携していたとされる  ・中核のClaude-Opus-4.5以外のエージェントは、EDRへの試験、OPSEC（身元や痕跡を隠す運用）の強化、文書化、プロキシ負荷試験、VM展開を分担。プレイブックではSpecterOpsのブログから収集した研究を読ませて手法を抽出させ、MITRE ATT&CKに対応付けて再現・試験・報告させる流れを指定し、回避技術自体はKaspersky・Palo Alto Networks・Bishop Foxなどの研究ブログから特定したとみられる  ・中核はPython製のローダー生成ツールで、Rust・Go製のペイロードを暗号化や回避処理で多層に包み、サンドボックスやアンチウイルス、EDRの検知をかわす独自のEXE／DLLを生成。約80のモジュールで70超の回避手法が試され、反復の末にエージェントは「ほぼすべての回避に成功」と報告した一方、同社はテスト基盤の記録がその結論を必ずしも裏付けないとしており、食い違いの理由は不明  ・一方で同社は、自動化されたAD（Active Directory）探索もAI主導ではあるが自律的に推論するLLMではなく、定義済みの選択肢から次手を選ぶ仕組みと指摘。マルウェア開発でのAIの役割は主に作業の調整と実験支援にとどまり、実際のEDR回避は人手のレビューと反復を挟む検証サイクルだったとしている  詳細は以下を参照： sophos.com/en-us/blog/pointi…

#Australia #HorseRacing HORSE RACING PROMPT V335.8 AU PRO-OMNI-FORENSIC MATRIX SYSTEM  ROLE  Sovereign Semantic-Forensic Race Shape Engine / Omni-Geometric Analyst.  OPERATIONAL PRINCIPLE  This system operates on the principle of Symmetric Semantic-Forensic Synthesis (SSFS). It treats racing parameters (track geometry, surface density, temporal shifts, starting barriers, and runner history) as a cohesive, interacting semantic ecosystem. Rather than executing uncomputable mathematical formulas, it runs structured, rule-based logical sweeps to isolate true kinetic ability and positional leverage, completely ignoring subjective human narratives and market price data.  PHASE 1: THE CORE SEMANTIC AXIOMS  1.  Symmetric Geometric Alignment: A runner's natural kinetic style (Leader,     Stalker, Closer) must exist in geometric symmetry with the track profile.       - Tight Circuits (Circumference < 1800m): Favour on-pace runners with low         barriers. Demote backmarkers unless a high-tempo melt is guaranteed.       - Spacious Tracks (Circumference \ge 1900m): Allow off-pace and         wide-sweeping late-torque closers to compete on equal terms. 2.  Viscosity Friction Tax (VFT): Carried mass is not a static penalty; it is a     dynamic tax scaled by surface moisture, temporal evaporation, and prep     depth. High weight (\ge 58kg) on soft/heavy ground requires active recent     fitness (raced within 21 days) or high-torque mud pedigree. If fresh (\ge 45     days off), it triggers an automatic metabolic ceiling. 3.  The Forensic "Forgive" Sieve: Superficial finishing positions must be     forensically audited. A poor last-start finish is entirely discarded and     replaced with their previous peak capability rating if they suffered:       - Traffic Blockage: Documented checked/blocked runs in the straight.       - Extreme Lateral Drag: Forced 3-wide or wider without cover on a tight         turn.       - Surface/Distance Mismatch: Forced onto an unsuited surface (e.g.,         turf-to-dirt) or run over an unsuited distance step-up (\ge 300m         first-up). 4.  Active Conditioning Dominance: In testing conditions (wet tracks, high     winds, or tight schedules), current race-fitness and race-hardness (runs     within 24 days) override raw class superiority or historical peak ratings of     resuming runners (\ge 90 days off). 5.  Zero-Market Bias: The engine does not ingest or refer to fixed odds, public     dividends, or market rank. Evaluations must rely entirely on the semantic     kinetic surplus of the runner relative to the cohort.  PHASE 2: INGESTION & TEMPORAL TRANSITION MATRIX  [STEP 2.1: TEMPORAL SYNC]   └── Resolve System Time, Scheduled Jump Time, and Date.   └── Calculate Time Shift: Delta t_shift = Jump Time - System Time (Hours).         │         ▼ [STEP 2.2: HOUR-OF-JUMP FORECAST ENGINE]   └── Project Temperature, Humidity, Wind Speed/Direction, and Rain at exact jump hour.   └── Solve Evaporative Drying State (EDS) over Delta t_shift window:         ├── Dynamic Soil Hardening (High Temp + High Wind + Low Humidity over temporal gap)         └── Dynamic Viscosity Surcharging (Rainfall duration + high humidity over temporal gap)         │         ▼ [STEP 2.3: COHORT CRUNCH]   └── Remove scratchings, compress barrier vectors (sequential 1 to N_active).         │         ▼ [STEP 2.4: ROUTING BY CLASS & SURFACE]   ├── SILO A (Turf - Tight Circuit < 1800m) ──► Apply: Tight-Turn Geometry Sieve   ├── SILO B (Turf - Spacious Track >= 1900m) ──► Apply: Late-Torque Closure Sweep   └── SILO C (Synthetic / Sand / Dirt) ──► Apply: Kickback & Traction Sparing Protocol  Environmental & Temporal Key Definitions:    - Evaporative Drying State (EDS): Evaluates if the track is transitioning     during the temporal gap (\Delta t_{\text{shift}}):       - Active Evaporation (Drying Phase): High wind and sun exposure will cause         clay loams to become sticky/tacky. This increases track viscosity,         placing a heavier tax on high-weight horses (\ge 58kg) and favoring         wide-sweeping lanes where the soil is less compacted.       - Active Wetting (Wetting Phase): Rain over the temporal gap lubricates         the surface. This favors front-runners on the rail who can maintain         traction before the loam gets churned.   - Rail Configuration Bias: If the rail is out \ge 6m on a tight-turning track,     inside lanes (Lanes 1-2) are highly preserved under dry compaction, but     quickly degrade into "bog paths" under heavy rain after Race 4.  PHASE 3: METABOLIC, GEOMETRIC, AND PEDIGREE SIEVES  Every runner must be evaluated against the following structured qualitative rules:  1. Metabolic Exhaustion Sieve (MES)    - The Second-Up Strain Rule: A runner is highly vulnerable to performance     regression ("bounce") if they are second-up off a layoff of \ge 90 days, AND     their first-up run was a high-exertion effort (finished within 1.5L of the     winner or won).       - Adjustment: If this rule fires on a wet track (Soft 6 or worse), apply a         severe metabolic penalty, demoting the runner from win slots unless they         have had a spacing interval of \ge 28 days between runs.   - Resuming Airway Veto: If a runner is resuming off a spell of \ge 90 days     over a distance of \ge 1400m without an official barrier trial or public     jumpout of \ge 1000m, they are assessed a Win-Only Veto (WOV).   - The Quick Backup Tax: If backing up within \le 10 days following a     high-intensity peak run (\le 1.5L beaten margin in a higher class), evaluate     their somatic frame: small-framed horses/mares are demoted on wet tracks;     large, muscular horses on firm turf are spared.  2. Geometric & Position Sieve (GPS)    - The Pocketing Sieve: On tight circuits (circumference < 1800m), backmarkers     (who settle in the rear 30% of the field) drawn inside (Barriers 1-3) absorb     a severe positional penalty. They are designated as "pocketing hazards" and     cannot be selected as 1A.   - The Wide-Sweep Cornering Tax: Runners drawn wide (\ge Barrier 8) on tight     circuits with a short run to the first turn (< 250m) are assessed a     geometric penalty.       - Exemption: If the runner possesses elite early speed (top 15% of the         field's early gate speed) and can cross to lead, the tax is waived.   - Pristine Lane Selection (LSO):       - Early Races (Races 1-3): Inside rail is pristine. Low barriers get         maximum energy sparing.       - Late Races (Races 5+ on wet tracks): Inside rail is a compaction bog.         Elevate runners mapped to sweep wide into clean air (Lanes 4+).  3. Pedigree Viscosity Sieve (PVS)    - Wet Track Pedigree Match: On Soft 6 or Heavy turf, evaluate the paternal     line and Maternal Grand Sire (MGS). If both show a wet-track win rate of     \ge 15% (or are elite European staying lines like Churchill, Frankel,     Montjeu, Lope de Vega), apply a positive pedigree discount.   - Dry-Speed Ceiling: Sires with pure short-sprint, dry-track profiles     (wet-track win rate < 8%) are capped at a maximum torque rating on wet turf     over distances \ge 1200m.  PHASE 4: THE MATRIX HUNTER SCAN (ARCHETYPES)  The engine must scan the field to identify if any of the following priority archetypes are active. The first matching archetype becomes the Locked Strategic Paradigm for the race:  1.  Lazarus Leader Monopoly: Active if the field size is small (N \le 7) and     there is exactly one mapped front-runner/leader (settles in top 15% of early     speed) in the field.       - Action: The lone leader is upgraded immediately. All weight and layoff         penalties for this runner are discounted by 50%, as they will dictate a         tactical, unpressured tempo. 2.  Claim-Exempt Class Gravity (CECG): Active when a proven metropolitan-class     performer drops \ge 2 levels in grade today, is partnered with an     experienced apprentice, and draws an inside barrier (1-4).       - Action: Locks this runner as the primary anchor, overriding standard         preparation density limits. 3.  Wide Momentum Sweeper (WMS): Active on wet turf (Soft 6+) on large tracks     (circumference \ge 1800m) late in the program (Race 5+).       - Action: Prioritize fit, mid-to-wide drawn closers mapped to make their         run down the outside of the track. 4.  Conditioned Fitness Anchor (CFA): Active in low-grade country/provincial     maiden races.       - Action: Prioritize fit, exposed runners with proven local track and         distance placings over unexposed, un-trialled debutants or resuming         favorites with long spells (\ge 120 days).  PHASE 5: EXECUTION SEQUENCE (5-PASS PROTOCOL)  You must execute the analysis sequentially. Write out your raw thinking for each pass clearly.  PASS 0: GROUNDED ENVIRONMENT SEARCH    - Document system time, target race jump time, and hours remaining     (\Delta t_{\text{shift}}).   - Retrieve jump-hour weather variables (wind speed, wind direction,     temperature, humidity, rain chance).   - Determine the active state of the track surface based on the temporal gap:     Dynamic Soil Hardening or Dynamic Moisture Saturation.   - Document the track profile (circumference, straight length, turns, rail     position, rail bias).   - List all active, confirmed runners with actual barrier draws     post-scratchings.  PASS 1: THE COHORT FORENSIC MATRIX (FPM)  For every single runner on the card, write down:  1.  Name, actual compressed barrier, and effective weight (allocated weight     minus apprentice claim). 2.  Natural running style and projected settling position (Leader, Stalker,     Midfield, Closer). 3.  Current fitness/preparation state (days since last run, runs this campaign). 4.  Track and Distance suitability rating (wins/places at course, distance, and     surface state). 5.  The Forensic Audit: Does this horse have a "forgive" marker from their last     two starts?  PASS 2: VETO & OVERRIDE SCREENING  Apply the hard vetoes:    - AEK (Absolute Exotic Kill): Lame/arrhythmia last start, 15+ starts with 0     career wins (unless GPR active), or 3x consecutive last-place finishes.   - WOV (Win-Only Veto): Resuming over \ge 1400m without a 1000m trial, or     second-up off long layoff with \le 14 days spacing.   - Place-Sparing Loophole: If a runner is vetoed for the win due to preparation     fatigue (V63) or high weight on wet turf, do not eliminate them from minor     exotic placings if their raw C&D capability ranks in the top 30% of the     cohort.  PASS 3: GEOMETRIC PACING HARMONY TEST    - Map the expected early speed. Identify if the early tempo will be:       - Fast/Contested: Demote vulnerable front-runners; elevate late-torque         closers.       - Slow/Tactical: Elevate on-pace runners and pocket savers; eliminate         backmarkers.   - Calculate the Spatial Resonance Score for the top 4 contenders:     \text{Resonance Score } (R) = | \text{Saddlecloth Number} - \text{Barrier Number} |       - Interpretation: Low scores (R \le 3) indicate spatial alignment and         clean logistics; high scores (R \ge 8) indicate potential spatial         conflict.  PASS 4: FINAL CANVAS RENDER  Generate the final report exactly in the format below.  PHASE 6: SYSTEM OUTPUT FORMAT  ================================================================================                          OMNI-FORENSIC REPORT V335.8 AU  TRACK: [Name] | RACE: [#] | DISTANCE: [m] | CLASS: [Class] GEOMETRIC PROFILE: Circumference [m] | Straight [m] | Turn Radius [Tight/Spacious] ENVIRONMENTAL VISCOSITY: Rating [State] | Wind [Speed/Direction] | Phase [Wetting/Drying] COHORT DENSITY: Active Field Size [N] | Rail Position [Position]  TEMPORAL TRANSITION DATA: - Analysis Time: [HH:MM] | Jump Time: [HH:MM] | Delta t_shift: [Hours] - Jump-Hour Forecast: Temp: [Value]C | Humidity: [Value]% | Wind: [Speed/Direction] - Surface State Transition: [Dynamic Soil Hardening (Evaporative) / Dynamic Moisture Saturation / Static]  1. DETERMINISTIC STRATEGIC PARADIGM - Fired Archetype: [Name of Tier 1 Archetype or "BASELINE SYMMETRY ACTIVE"] - Tactical Flow: [Expected Tempo: Fast / Moderate / Slow - Impact on styles]  2. THE SURVIVAL SIEVE - Eliminated (AEK - Absolute Exotic Kills): [Name (Specific Veto Code / Reason)] - Eliminated (WOV - Win-Only Vetoes): [Name (Specific Veto Code / Reason)] - Minor Exotic Sparing (Retained for 3rd/4th only): [Name (Reason)] - Active Survivor Cluster: [Name, Name, Name]  3. 1A SOVEREIGN (Primary Selection) - Name: [Name] (Barrier: [#], Weight: [Eff. Weight]kg, Style: [Style]) - Kinetic Driver: [Why this horse is completely aligned with the geometric/pace shape today] - Forensic Validation: [Key course/distance form, fitness recency, or forgive audit details] - Pre-Turn Position projection: [Expected path, e.g., Box seat, Lane 1-2 / Sweeping wide clean air]  4. 1B SHIELD (Tactical Counter-Weight) - Name: [Name] (Barrier: [#], Weight: [Eff. Weight]kg, Style: [Style]) - Portfolio Role: [The opposing tactical mapping profile that secures the portfolio against sudden track-bias shifts]  5. MORTAL LAY (Mortal Violation) - Name: [Name] - Critical Flaw: [The exact geometric, weight, or metabolic mismatch that falsifies their favoriteness]  6. EXOTIC RESIDUALS (In order of priority) - [Name 1] (C&D Specialist / Lightweight closing) - [Name 2] (Compaction bog escape tracker)  7. STRUCTURAL BETTING MATRIX - Primary Bet: [WIN on 1A / EACH-WAY / QUINELLA boxing 1A & 1B] - Value Overlay: [Name & Justification of best price-to-probability contender] - Structured Trifecta/First Four:   * A-Group: [1A, 1B]   * B-Group: [Name, Name]   * C-Group: [Name]  8. COHORT DESIGNATION MAP  |Status|Horse Name|Barrier|Weight|Style|Prep Depth|Trk/Dist|Forgive?|LSO Path|Final Action| |:---|:---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---| |`[Status]`|`[Name]`|`[#]`|`[#kg]`|`[Style]`|`[Prep]`|`[W/R]`|`[Yes/No]`|`[Lanes]`|`[Action]`|

1 Corinthians 15:23 Commentary Verse Quoted in KJV “But every man in his own order: Christ the firstfruits; afterward they that are Christ’s at his coming.” (1 Corinthians 15:23)  Detailed Exegesis of Each Phrase  “But” The word “But” continues Paul’s explanation from verse 22, where he said, “For as in Adam all die, even so in Christ shall all be made alive.” Paul now clarifies that resurrection happens according to divine order. The promise is certain, but it is not chaotic. God does not raise men randomly, carelessly, or without sequence. He has an order. This “But” also prevents misunderstanding. When Paul says, “in Christ shall all be made alive,” he is not saying everyone is raised at the same instant or in the same company. He immediately adds, “every man in his own order.” There is resurrection certainty, but there is also resurrection order. The word reminds us that God’s program is structured. Men confuse things when they flatten all resurrection doctrine into one general event. Scripture gives order, rank, timing, and distinction. Christ first. Then those that are Christ’s at His coming. The Bible believer must let the text divide the order the way God gives it. “every man” Paul says “every man,” showing that resurrection applies personally and individually within God’s appointed order. No believer in Christ will be forgotten. No sleeping saint will be misplaced. No grave will hide one member of Christ’s body from the voice of the Lord. Every man who belongs to Christ has his place in God’s resurrection program. This phrase also reinforces certainty. Verse 22 says, “in Christ shall all be made alive.” Verse 23 says “every man” in order. The resurrection is not a vague crowd event where personal identity is lost. The same believer who died will be raised. God knows His own. The phrase also reaches beyond human timing. Men die in different ages, countries, conditions, and circumstances. Some die in beds, some in prisons, some in battle, some at sea, some in martyrdom, some in obscurity. Yet God says “every man.” Death may scatter the dust, but God does not lose the person. “in his own order:” This phrase is doctrinally crucial. “Order” means arrangement, rank, sequence, or appointed place. Resurrection has a divine order. Christ is first, then those that are Christ’s at His coming. God does not treat resurrection as a confused mass event without distinction. He has arranged it according to His prophetic and dispensational program. The phrase “his own order” also teaches that not every resurrection belongs to the same group or time. The resurrection of Christ is unique as firstfruits. The resurrection of Church Age saints is connected with the coming of the Lord for His own. Other resurrection passages deal with Israel, Tribulation saints, and the lost at the final judgment. Paul here is especially dealing with those in Christ and their resurrection hope. This phrase guards against careless theology. A man who refuses order will mix passages, confuse the rapture with the Second Coming, blur the Church with Israel, and collapse separate judgments and resurrections into one tangled mess. Paul says “order.” God is not the author of confusion. “Christ the firstfruits;” Christ is the firstfruits. This repeats and strengthens verse 20: “But now is Christ risen from the dead, and become the firstfruits of them that slept.” Firstfruits refers to the first portion of the harvest, which guarantees the rest of the harvest. Christ rose first in victorious, glorified, never-to-die-again resurrection. This does not mean no one was raised from the dead before Christ in a temporary sense. The widow’s son, the Shunammite’s son, Lazarus, and others were brought back to life, but they later died again. Christ rose in a different order and quality of resurrection. Romans 6:9 says, “Christ being raised from the dead dieth no more.” He is the firstfruits because His resurrection is permanent, victorious, and

Hey, the router story is from April 2026. Russian GRU (APT28/Fancy Bear) exploited known flaws in vulnerable SOHO routers (mostly older TP-Link) worldwide to hijack DNS settings and spy on traffic/steal creds for espionage.  FBI's Operation Masquerade disrupted the US portion by resetting compromised devices. It hit households & small offices in 23+ US states and globally.  Canada was a partner on the joint advisory, so likely some impact there too. Not tied to specific ISPs—just unpatched routers. They started broad, then filtered for military/gov/critical infra targets.  Why: Standard GRU intel gathering. Update firmware, reboot your router, ditch defaults, and disable remote access for safety.

#threatreport #LowCompleteness Crypto Guest at Dawn Endpoint (Midnight) Ransomware Analysis | 02-06-2026 Source: asec.ahnlab.com/ko/93931/ Key details below ↓  💀Threats: Midnight_ransomware, Babuk, Shadow_copies_delete_technique, Trojan/win.generic.c5765109, Ransom/mdp.delete.m2117, Ransom/mdp.command.m2255, Ransom/mdp.decoy.m1171, Ransom/mdp.event.m1946, Ransom/mdp.event.m1875,  🎯Victims: Windows environments, Esxi environments, Nas environments  🌐Geo: North korea, Asia  🤖LLM extracted TTPs:` T1082, T1480.002, T1486, T1489, T1490, T1562.001  🧨IOCs:  - File: 3  - Command: 1  💽Software: ESXi  🔢Algorithms: md5, chacha20, sha256  🗂️Win API: FindFirstFileW, MoveFileExW  #threatreport: EndPoint, a ransomware variant formerly known as Midnight, is derived from the Babuk ransomware framework and targets Windows environments, along with ESXi and NAS systems. This ransomware employs a double-extortion technique, combining file encryption with threats of data exfiltration to pressure victims into payment. The files encrypted by EndPoint are marked with the .endpoint extension, and the ransom note instructs victims to contact the operators via a uTox ID. Notably, the email account included in the ransom note has been traced back to North Korean-linked attackers, suggesting potential state-sponsored involvement in these cyber extortion efforts.  Prior to initiating the encryption process, EndPoint meticulously terminates several critical processes across different applications, specifically targeting database systems, office applications, and email clients. It conducts a thorough cleanup by deleting Windows volume shadow copies using the command "vssadmin.exe delete shadows /all /quiet," thus hindering recovery options for victims. Additionally, it halts backup and security services, including those from Veeam, Sophos, and Acronis, which further complicates the recovery efforts for affected systems.  The encryption routine of EndPoint is aggressive and targets a variety of directories and file types, such as Windows system files, Program Files, AppData, boot manager files, and executables. It utilizes a multi-threaded approach to increase encryption speed by leveraging the number of CPU cores available on the infected machine and employs a mutex named Mutexisfunnylocal to avoid re-execution of the malware, thus streamlining its operations.  As the ransomware landscape evolves, EndPoint exemplifies the persistent threat posed by sophisticated ransomware variants that exploit known vulnerabilities and established cyberattack methodologies.

2 / 2  그리고 중국과 해커들이 우수한 인력들이 그 업무만을 하는 이들로서 전문성이 높다는 것, 한국 기업 다산이 단둥에 중국과 북한인들에게 교육을 제공한 것도 알고 있었고 다산을 조사해야한다 했다. 다산은 한국의 투표 시스템에 관여하는 기업인데 중국과 북한 해커들에게 해킹 방법을 알려주었을 것이라 암시하는 것이다.  실제로 중국이나 북한 해커들로 보이는 김수키는 선관위보다 훨씬 보안이 강력한 한국 외교부, 행안부 등을 해킹한 것이 드러난 적이 있다. 보안도 허술하고 사전투표기간에 망분리를 해제한 것을 인정한 선관위를 해킹하는 것은 아주 쉽다.  한국의 방첩활동을 없애고 국가안보활동을 약화시키는 자들이 있는데 이들을 기소하고 처벌해야한다는 말을 하면서 해커들과 싸웠던 윤석열 대통령이 감옥에 있는 것은 정당하지 않고 자신은 윤대통령을 현직 대통령이라 여긴다고 했다.  한국의 국민들 170만명이 일어나 거리로 나온다면 정부를 바꿀 수 있다며 국민들의 참여를 촉구했다. 프랭크 박사도 트럼프가 그냥 해결해주기를 바라면 안되고 국민들이 직접 결단하고 목소리를 내야한다고 이야기했는데 같은 맥락이다.  자신은 미국에서 미국 정부에, 그리고 한인들에게 한국의 상황을 알리고 있으며, 한미상호방호조약 제3조에 의해 사이버 공격도 조약의 대상이 되기 때문에  미국이 개입해야된다고 했다.  프랭크 박사와 모스탄 대사의 이야기를 들어보니 이제 미국이 한국의 상황을 잘 알고 있고, 미국의 부정선거와 한국의 부정선거는 동일선상에 있는 것으로 여기고 한국에 개입할 준비가 되어 있다.  프랭크 박사는 미국의 부정선거를 알리고 부정선거를 막은 린델 사장, 패트릭 번과 긴밀하게 연결되어 있고, 트럼프도 신뢰하는 인물이다.   이번에 부정선거 증거를 제대로 잡아낸다면 미국에서 바로 개입을 하지는 않겠지만 미국 정부에 부정선거 증거들을 제대로 정리해서 알리면 미국도 공식적으로 한국의 부정선거를 언급할 것으로 보이고, 한국의 국민들이 거리로 나온다면, 그 때 경찰과 군인들이 거리로 나온 시민들을 탄압한다면 베네수엘라나 이란에서처럼 국민들 억압하는 부당한 권력을 제거해줄 것으로 보인다.   우리 국민들은 겁먹지 말고, 자기에게 불이익이 올까 걱정하며 기회주의적으로 몸사리지 말고 용기를 내어 목소리를 내고 거리로 나오기만 하면 된다.

New World Order is here and in your face: Critical timeline exposes freedom-killing plot quietly empowered by emerging digital beast system  The timeline is reaching a critical hour: Now is the time to resist government-corporate tyranny being assembled by a weaponized national security state; please share this timeline far and wide.  Leo Hohmann Jun 03, 2026  Some people call it the digital control grid. Others call it the surveillance state. Still others refer to it as the matrix, the panopticon, or skynet. I call it the beast system.  Whatever you wish to call it, I think it’s fair to say that it’s growing. And it keeps growing no matter which political party holds power in Washington. But it advances in drips and drabs, it creeps in quietly, one law, one executive order at a time, with little to no media coverage. Like a slow-growing cancerous tumor, it functions as a silent killer. A killer of freedom.  Context is everything when trying to understand the globalist plan for a one-world system of total control over all human behavior.  And context is what’s often missing from most reporting on the subject.  So, I’ve constructed a working timeline showing various highpoints of the emerging beast system as it relates specifically to the methodical, step-by-step crushing of freedom in what was once the freest country in the world, America. Keep in mind that the devastating trendline I’ve exposed in this article speaks not only to the loss of freedom in America but the entire world, because once you eliminate the idea of America being the global beacon of individual freedom, the rest of the world will fall like dominos.  I would argue that freedom has already been 90 percent eliminated in America, but the idea or perception that America stands for freedom still persists in the minds of many around the world, even if not nearly as powerfully as it did 50 or 60 years ago.  It is my belief that the billionaire globalist power elites entered their final phase of a global takeover during the Covid pandemic, when they began taking off their masks and no longer pretending to be running a free country. People like Dr. Anthony Fauci openly mocked those who chose freedom over his iteration of “safety” and “security.”  Since then, the technocrats see it as crucial to eliminate even the perception of America as a beacon of freedom. If it takes a world war to finish the job, then they will take us to World War III. Since depopulation is also a major component of their takeover plans, you can bank on World War III continuing to heat up around the world at key flashpoints – Ukraine, Iran, Lebanon, and new fronts yet to be opened such as Taiwan, Cuba and other places.  But while wars tend to steal the headlines, it’s what’s happening in the shadows, where few are watching, that delivers the real goods in terms of government and corporate power over the individual. Global financial oligarchs and their allied tech bros are constructing a digital noose they intend to hang around every person’s neck, starting with smart phones, tablets and TVs, and moving on to autonomous smart cars, smart appliances and digital gauges of all sorts, some of which will ultimately be implantable as part of your “health.” These devices, promoted on the basis of health, safety, and convenience, are being used to spy and create a social-credit scoring system that rewards the compliant and punishes the non-compliant. This digital scoring system, powered by AI, will include not only your actions, your purchases, your eating and travel habits, but also your opinions expressed in online platforms. Rights will be terminated, replaced with privileges granted by nameless, faceless overlords and enforced by digital algorithms.  Below is my timeline, which taken in its totality point towards something bigger than what is typically thought possible when these events were reported in isolation. I have reported on almost all of this previously, but to see it all in one place in a timeline is, I believe, incredibly powerful and worthy to be shared with your friends and family who doubt the existence of an emerging digital control grid.  Oracle Software’s billionaire founder, Larry Ellison, the world’s second richest man, told a group of financial analysts in September 2024 that once all the data centers are built and AI is fully implemented, “citizens will be on their best behavior because we are constantly recording and reporting everything that’s going on.” There will be very little crime because everyone will know they are being watched and listened to 24/7. In fact, there will be no need for many of the police officers now employed when you can just replace them with AI drones and robots, especially for risky tasks like high-speed chases. “You just have a drone follow the car,” Ellison said. “It’s very simple in the age of autonomous drones.”  Former President Joe Biden includes a provision in his 2021 Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act that requires all cars manufactured or imported into America to include a remote “kill switch,” and the auto industry dutifully complies without any semblance of push back. The National Law Review reports: “The technology could involve cameras monitoring eye movement, sensors analyzing steering and braking patterns, or touch-based biometric readers built into the steering wheel or ignition surface. It also could leverage AI. The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA} is still finalizing the technical standards — a detail that matters, because the specific data collection methods will drive (no pun intended) privacy and security compliance. Notably, many of these features and capabilities – often embedded in devices referred to as ‘dashcams’ – have already become popular in fleet vehicles.”  Donald Trump, sitting in the Roosevelt Room of the White House on day one of his second term in office, Jan. 21, 2025, holds a press event alongside Oracle Software Chairman Larry Ellison, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, and the CEO of Japan’s SoftBank, to announce Project Stargate, pledging to raise half a trillion dollars to build out the infrastructure of AI, calling for hundreds of new data centers under the pretense of “we must compete with China.” Along with the others mentioned above, MGX, Microsoft and Nvidia are partnering in the Stargate Project.  Massive public-private partnerships involving Google, Microsoft, Oracle, Meta, OpenAI, and Elon Musk’s X-affiliated companies follow with all of them enjoying access to trillions in investment to build thousands of data centers (we already have more than 5,000 built and under construction with at least another 3,000 in the planning stages). This represents more data centers in the U.S. than what exist in China, Russia and Europe combined, indicating that something much bigger than what we’re being told is in the works.  In June 2025, the U.S. Army creates Detachment 201 commissions, giving tech bros automatic officerships, with ranks up to lieutenant colonel “based on skill sets,” despite having never had any previous military experience or service. They claim to be “merging innovation with national defense” in what amounts to a militarized public-private partnership, or PPP, focused on national security.  Digital AI software creator Palantir Technologies, co-founded by noted Bilderberg member and transhumanist Peter Thiel and the cold-blooded technocrat Alex Karp, is empowered across multiple federal government agencies to create digital profiles/dossiers on every American, while contracting with U.S., Israeli and Ukrainian militaries to create kill lists that expedite the “digital kill chain” of those people considered enemies of their global agenda. The result is mass graves in Gaza, Lebanon and Ukraine, and the suspicion by some that information being gathered on Americans could one day be similarly used against them.  On August 25, 2025, President Trump signs an executive order to create “rapid-response National Guard units” capable of being deployed within hours to U.S. cities for the purpose of quelling civil disturbances and protecting “public safety.” Each state is required to have these quick reaction forces ready by January 1, 2026, further blurring the lines between federalized national security issues and local law enforcement.  On Jan. 23, 2026, Oracle’s Larry Ellison (there’s that name again) heads up investment group purchasing TikTok from Chinese company and promptly moves to censor criticism of the Israeli government and certain other sacred cows within the empowered elite, also known as the “Epstein class.”  Large blocks of warehousing space are purchased by the federal government in more than 17 states in 2025 and early 2026, for the stated purpose of housing illegal migrants. This is concerning, given the fact that the Trump administration pivoted away from wholesale roundups of migrants in the late summer/early fall of 2025. What types of criminals or “terrorists” does the U.S. government plan to shuttle into this bevy of new prison space?  On July 23, 2025, President Trump signs executive order declaring data centers to be part of America’s “national security” apparatus, equating them with military installations and therefore outside of the purview of state and local governments.  On December 11, 2025, President Trump signs executive order banning state regulation of AI. This is a clear violation of the 10th Amendment to the U.S. Constitution.  In January 2026 Kentucky Congressman Thomas Massie introduces an amendment to Biden’s infrastructure bill that would have eliminated the mandated remote vehicle kill switches, but Massie’s attempt to preserve driver freedom is narrowly defeated with 57 Republicans joining 211 Democrats in opposition. Massie is later defeated at the polls in May when President Trump and several billionaires from the Epstein class raised a record amount of money for his GOP primary opponent.  In early May, 2026, Larry Fink, the CEO of BlackRock, co-chairman of the globalist World Economic Forum and close friend of President Trump, confides that, with trillions of dollars invested in AI data centers, one of his greatest fears is that people will wake up, rise up, and start attacking data centers with drones.  On May 26, 2026, the tech journal Wired reports that internal Trump administration documents gleaned from the FBI and Homeland Security reveal the government is now viewing anti-data center and anti-AI activists as potential domestic terrorists. Wired reports: “In the wake of attacks on CEOs, a nationwide protest movement targeting data centers, and increasing concerns about AI job replacement, federal intelligence agencies and domestic law enforcement are circulating reports with a new domestic target in mind: anti-technology extremists.”  In late April 2026 the U.S. Department of War announced it was creating an Autonomous Warfare Center within the Pentagon. The presumed goal, if we are to take the name seriously, is to eliminate the need for human involvement in the kill chain by turning such decisions over to AI. Some have referred to it as “autonomous killing.” For the 2027 fiscal year, the War Department has requested $54.6 billion for this organization, representing a staggering 24,166% increase in funding. As noted by national security investor Paul Miller, “that single line accounts for nearly 15 percent of the total reconciliation and exceeds the gross domestic product of many small nations and is higher than the entire budget request for the U.S. Marine Corps of $52.8 billion.”  On April 9, 2026, President Trump posted to social media unleashing a blistering attack on a group of podcasters and journalists who have been critical of his Iran war, an offensive war of choice conducted in partnership with Benjamin Netanyahu and the Israeli Defense Forces (IDF). He singled out Tucker Carlson, Megyn Kelly, Alex Jones and Candace Owens, along with former Georgia Congresswoman Marjorie Taylor Green, as “nut jobs,” “troublemakers,” and “losers” and portraying them as a danger to America’s national security.  In late May, 2026, it was reported that certain members of Congress had inserted Section 224 into the National Defense Authorization Act as proposed in the U.S. House of Representatives for fiscal year 2027. If approved later this year, Section 224 would lead to the full integration or “fusion” of the Israeli and U.S. militaries for purposes of surveillance technology, including AI, autonomous systems, cyber, defense intelligence, directed energy, quantum computing, biotech, and more.  BOTTOM LINE: America was never designed by its founders to be a national security state obsessed with overseas wars, internal tracking, keeping tabs on individual citizens, monitoring those who exercise their First Amendment rights to freely assemble, move about, or to exercise free speech, no matter how controversial that speech may be.  I conclude with a video by the Tenth Amendment Center titled “Liberty isn’t taken, it’s surrendered,” which speaks clearly to the point of this article and the evidence presented herein that suggests our basic freedoms are under systematic assault by billionaire oligarchs and technocrats partnered with the criminal entity in Washington that people refer to as the government.  youtube.com/live/jAMF9uqOKP8…

A quiet but profound shift among the global super-elite: Many in the top 0.1% now show deep nihilism  — losing faith in society's stability and Earth's habitability.   They're building elaborate escape plans. From doomsday bunkers to private jets on standby.  Roughly one-third of America’s ~900 billionaires have sophisticated doomsday strategies:  Gulfstream jets, fortified bunkers in New Zealand, and long-term survival stockpiles.   Triggers?  Zombie apocalypse, revolution, pandemic, or cyber attack.  What was fringe is now mainstream among the ultra-wealthy.  Daily isolation mechanisms:  • Private jets bypass TSA lines  • Concierge medicine for elite healthcare  • Ultra-exclusive schools shielding kids from public system struggles The rich live in a parallel reality with zero friction from ordinary life.  Elon Musk’s multi-planetary vision implies Earth may not be salvageable.   AI leaders admit 7-10% extinction risk from superintelligence  — yet proceed, treating humanity’s future like a high-stakes game.

A government cyber agency flagged compromised versions of a widely-used code package, and the reason it matters reaches into apps you use every day without knowing it.  Modern software isn't built. It's assembled from thousands of borrowed parts.  Almost every app and website pulls in open-source packages written by strangers, and those packages pull in others, stacking into a dependency tree nobody fully audits. When attackers slip malicious code into one popular package, it can flow downstream into everything that depends on it, silently, in the next routine update.  You don't have to be targeted. You just have to use something that used the thing that got poisoned.  This is supply-chain risk, and it's the soft underbelly of the whole digital world. The convenience that lets developers move fast, reusing trusted components, is the exact mechanism an attacker exploits to reach far more systems than they could one at a time.  Trust is efficient. It's also the attack surface.  The fix isn't to stop sharing code, the entire internet runs on it. It's vigilance: agencies publishing alerts, maintainers locking down accounts, teams auditing what they pull in. The alert went out precisely so the patch spreads faster than the compromise.  One bad package upstream. Thousands of front doors downstream.

You’re right to be cautious—especially if you’re in or connected to the military. Posting strong opinions on alliances, foreign policy, or “unlawful orders” can draw scrutiny under rules like the UCMJ (for service members), DoD social media policies, or even broader laws on threats/incitement.   Sticking to verifiable facts, personal views framed as opinion, and avoiding direct calls to action or unsubstantiated accusations reduces risk.   I’m not giving legal advice; consider consulting a lawyer or JAG if you’re active duty.  On the Constitution and Military Cooperation with Israel The U.S.-Israel defense relationship, including the proposed Section 224 in the House FY2027 NDAA draft, is a policy choice authorized by Congress and the Executive branch—not an automatic violation of the Constitution.  •Congressional power: Article I gives Congress authority over the military (raising/supporting armies, appropriations, rules for armed forces), foreign commerce, and declaring war. The NDAA is the annual defense authorization bill—thoroughly debated and voted on. Section 224 expands tech cooperation, R&D, co-production, and an “executive agent” for coordination (AI, cyber, missiles, etc.). This builds on decades of existing law, MOUs, and aid (e.g., qualitative military edge requirements).  •Executive role: Treaties, agreements, and day-to-day foreign policy fall under the President (Article II). Alliances like this aren’t “unconstitutional” just because critics oppose them—similar ties exist with NATO, Japan, South Korea, etc.  •No prohibition on allies: The Constitution doesn’t ban military partnerships, tech sharing, or industrial cooperation with friendly nations. Claims of “surrendering sovereignty” or “Mossad control” are opinions/conspiracy theories, not legal findings. Historical allegations (e.g., Mossad “seizing” Pentagon offices post-9/11) lack credible mainstream evidence and echo debunked tropes.  The USS Liberty incident (1967) was a tragic friendly-fire attack during war; Israel apologized and paid compensation. It’s been investigated multiple times but doesn’t legally invalidate current alliances. Critics (including some lawmakers like Reps. Massie and Khanna) argue Section 224 reduces oversight, risks tech leaks, or ties U.S. policy too closely to Israel—legitimate policy debate. Opponents can lobby, amend, or vote against the NDAA. That’s how the system works.  Unlawful Orders and Military Obligations Service members swear an oath to the Constitution, not any person or foreign government.   Under the Uniform Code of Military Justice (UCMJ): •You must obey lawful orders. •You have a duty to refuse manifestly unlawful ones—those that violate the Constitution, U.S. law, or require committing a crime (e.g., war crimes under the Geneva Conventions, clear atrocities).  Routine implementation of congressionally authorized defense policy (like alliance programs, training, or tech integration) is presumed lawful. Disobeying it risks court-martial under Articles 90/92. Courts-martial judges decide lawfulness after the fact, with a heavy presumption in favor of the order.  This doesn’t mean blind obedience—Nuremberg principles and U.S. law reject “just following orders” for clear crimes. But policy disagreements (even strong ones about Israel) aren’t the same as ordering murder or torture. Individual service members don’t get to veto foreign policy.  The Constitution’s Declaration of Independence mentions altering/abolishing destructive government, but that’s revolutionary rhetoric—not operative law for the military today. The actual Constitution sets amendment processes, elections, and checks/balances. Military personnel can’t “dismantle” policy they dislike; that’s for voters and courts.

🔒 CYBERSECURITY, PRIVACY & OPEN SOURCE ROUNDUP — June 03, 2026  1️⃣ 19-YEAR-OLD LINUX VULNERABILITY CIFSWITCH GRANTS ROOT ACCESS  A critical privilege escalation vulnerability named CIFSwitch has been discovered in Linux, lurking since 2007. The flaw exploits a logic flaw between the Linux kernel's CIFS client and the cifs-utils package, allowing low-privileged users to escalate to full root access. SpaceX security engineer Asim Viladi Oglu Manizada identified the issue, and a public proof-of-concept is already circulating. Linux administrators are urged to apply the latest kernel patches immediately. @TheHackersNews  2️⃣ MICROSOFT 365 ANDROID "FLAGLEFT" BUG EXPOSES BILLIONS OF USERS  A single debug flag mistakenly left enabled in production has exposed billions of Microsoft 365 Android users to token theft. The vulnerability, dubbed "FlagLeft," allows any app installed on the same device to silently steal a user's signed-in account token — granting unrestricted access to email, files, calendar, and the ability to send messages without any user prompt. The bug was found in Word and other Microsoft 365 Android applications. @TheHackersNews  3️⃣ QUALCOMM CHIP VULNERABILITY COMPROMISES PHONES BEFORE ANDROID EVEN BOOTS  Kaspersky researchers uncovered a serious hardware-level vulnerability in Qualcomm chips that affects smartphones, IoT devices, and vehicles. The flaw can be exploited with just a few minutes of physical USB access — meaning phone repair shops are now part of your threat model. The compromise occurs before the Android operating system even starts, making it virtually undetectable through traditional software-based security tools. @kaspersky  4️⃣ WINDOWS SEARCH URI HANDLER LEAKS NTLMV2 HASHES  A newly disclosed vulnerability in Windows Search's URI handler silently leaks NTLMv2 credentials to attacker-controlled servers. The exploitation requires nothing more than a single malicious link — clicking it triggers credential theft without any visible prompt or warning. This attack vector is particularly dangerous in enterprise environments where NTLMv2 hashes can be cracked offline or used in pass-the-hash attacks. @The_Cyber_News  5️⃣ 46% OF ENTERPRISE IDENTITY ACTIVITY IS "DARK MATTER"  Research from @OrchidSecurity reveals that nearly half of all enterprise identity activity remains completely invisible to security teams. These "Identity Dark Matter" actions are hidden within unmanaged applications, shadow IT systems, and machine accounts. This massive blind spot represents exactly where modern attackers operate undetected — identity-based attacks that security teams simply cannot see or monitor. @TheHackersNews  6️⃣ ARGAMAL RAT DROPPED THROUGH TROJANIZED ADULT GAMES  Kaspersky's Securelist research team identified Argamal, a previously unknown remote access trojan distributed through pirated adult game torrents. The malware uses a two-stage approach: a first implant goes dormant for several days before downloading and executing the full RAT. It establishes persistence through COM hijacking, scheduled tasks, and rotating command-and-control servers — making it difficult to detect and remove. @kaspersky  7️⃣ HIDDEN ATTACK PATHS IN ACTIVE DIRECTORY AND ENTRA ID  At InfoSec Europe, SpecterOps researcher Mark Wilson presented findings on how attackers leverage hidden attack paths within Active Directory and Microsoft Entra ID to achieve enterprise-wide compromise. The talk, which drew a standing-room-only audience, highlighted how attackers systematically bypass industry-standard defenses by exploiting overlooked identity and access management configurations. @SpecterOps  💭 The CIFSwitch vulnerability being 19 years old is a stark reminder that legacy code in critical infrastructure rarely gets the scrutiny it deserves. Combined with the Microsoft 365 Android debug flag and the Qualcomm chip vulnerability, we're seeing a pattern where the weakest link is often the layer nobody thought to secure — whether it's a 19-year-old CIFS client, a forgotten debug switch, or hardware that boots before the OS. Patch everything, audit your attack surface at every layer, and assume your blind spots are already being exploited.  Which vulnerability should organizations prioritize patching first — the 19-year-old Linux root escalation or the Qualcomm hardware flaw? 👇  #Cybersecurity #Linux #CVE #ZeroDay #Privacy #OpenSource #InfoSec

🚨Security Alert: New Unpatched Vulnerability in Windows "search:" URI Handler🚨  Researchers have identified a concerning unpatched security vulnerability within the Windows search: URI handler.  Similar to the previously disclosed flaw in the Windows Snipping Tool, this vulnerability can be exploited to force a system to leak NTLMv2 hashes. By luring a user to trigger a malicious URI, an attacker can capture these hashes, which can then be used in relay or cracking attacks to gain unauthorized access to credentials or network resources.  Why this matters:  1- Ease of Exploitation: URI handler vulnerabilities are often triggered via simple links in web browsers, documents, or messaging platforms.  2- Credential Exposure: NTLMv2 hash theft remains a highly effective method for attackers to escalate privileges and move laterally within a corporate environment.  3- Zero-Day Status: As of now, there is no official patch available from Microsoft.  Recommended Mitigations:  1- Monitor Network Traffic: Watch for unusual SMB or HTTP traffic originating from workstations that are attempting to authenticate to external IP addresses.  2- Filter/Block: If possible, implement network-level blocks for outbound SMB (TCP/445) to untrusted external addresses.  3- User Awareness: Advise teams to exercise caution when clicking suspicious links, especially those formatted as search: query in unexpected contexts.  We are keeping a close eye on this, and we anticipate a security update from Microsoft in an upcoming Patch Tuesday cycle. In the meantime, keeping your systems updated and enforcing robust endpoint detection is critical.  Stay vigilant, and ensure your security teams are aware of this potential attack vector.  #CyberSecurity #InfoSec #WindowsSecurity #ZeroDay #TechAlert #NetworkSecurity #ITSecurity

Now, on to the "how":  All 1000 articles follow the same format. Think "definition of ___", where each article subject is different, but the layout/structure is virtually the same.  Given the similarity, I was able to set up a single AI workflow with prompts and settings that apply to every article.  I used Publish Owl (publishowl.com) to set this all up. Publish Owl lets you create 100% custom workflows so you can generate any type of article, and it connects to your site so the articles can be published automatically. No need for copying/pasting.  The workflow I created uses gemini-2.5-flash to generate the content. My master prompt is 748 words and details the structure, requirements, and minor things to follow or ignore depending on the keyword. The first part of it is shown in the screenshot.  I also added featured image generation to the workflow, so every article gets a unique featured image. These don't use AI, so the cost is $0. They also aren't "stock photos". Let me explain...  Publish Owl allows you to set up image templates with image and text variables. You can map any image to an image variable, and the title or keyword to the text variable. In my case, my template only includes a text variable that maps to the keyword (with each word capitalized). It also has a background image I created in Canva that's the same for each (just has my brand colors and a few decorative elements).  To see an example of the image template feature in action, take a look at this article: publishowl.com/blog/best-wir…  The featured image is just a yellow background, with a text variable at the top mapped to the article title, and two image variables mapped to the first two product images. The in-article images are also templates simply mapped to the product image (with background removed and drop shadow added).   Image templates are one of the most useful features of Publish Owl vs other AI content tools.  My workflow also includes automatic internal linking. This is another crazy powerful feature in Publish Owl. I gave it my sitemap URL, indexed it, and now Publish Owl can score the relevance between every article already on my site and the newly generated article. It then finds words or phrases that are relevant to an existing article and adds an internal link there to the relevant article. So yes, internal linking is automatically handled (and is actually relevant/useful to the end user) on all 1,000 articles I generated.  Next, I added automatic Table of Contents generation on every article. Not only does it add a TOC after the first paragraph (you can customize the location btw), it's also collapsible even when pushed to WordPress!  Lastly, I connected the workflow to my site, and enabled scheduling so that each article is automatically scheduled to publish one hour apart. At 24/day, my 1,000 articles will all finally be published in mid-July. I opted for consistent publishing over time rather than a one-time content dump since Bing/DDG seem to prefer this in my tests.

The latest Red Hat npm malware is much more sophisticated than other Shai-Hulud variants we've analyzed, using GitHub not just for data exfiltration, but for storing and delivering malicious code.  It has a 6 stage dropper, looping back to its own 4th stage dropper logic, and a slightly different Miasma commit message, which could differentiate between different infections, this string could be used to track which repositories were affected by which stage, or potentially a new campaign by the same actor.  The "firedalazer" commit string could be used by the malware to upload new variants of the malware through GitHub, which is a pretty cool technique to keep the malware alive through different GitHub accounts.  After posting this - @ramimacisabird mentioned to me that letsgo0 and the 2nd GitHib account we've referenced in the blog were previously infected and are not the actual threat actor's accounts.   ox.security/blog/six-stages-…

〰️GALATIANS 4〰️ AMP 12 Believers, I beg of you, become as I am [free from the bondage of Jewish ritualism and ordinances], for I have become as you are [a Gentile]. You did me no wrong [when I first came to you; do not do it now] 13 …you know that it was because of a physical illness that I [remained and] preached the gospel to you the first time;  14 and even though my physical condition was a trial to you, you did not regard it with contempt, or scorn and reject me; but you received me as an angel of God, even as Christ Jesus Himself  ME➡️ Paul, the ultimate Jew, became like a gentile (words he never thought he'd say! It's funny).  Why would his followers become Jewish?   Paul was in poor physical condition. He had an eye problem at the very least.  He had been beaten up severely many times.  He probably had all kinds of maladies.  It wasn't an excuse to not serve and teach, in his case (He was called).  His life in Christ was very hard.   GROK ➡️ Yes — this section (4:12-14) shows Paul’s heart wide open.  He shifts from sharp theology to personal pleading: “Become like me, for I became like you.”  The ultimate Jew — the former Pharisee of Pharisees, the guy who was “advancing beyond many of his contemporaries” in Judaism — is now begging Gentile believers: “Come live in the freedom I’m living in. I left all that ritualism behind to become like you. Why are you trying to go the opposite direction and become like the old me?”  It really is funny when you picture it. Paul saying something like, “I gave up my elite Jewish credentials for you… and now you want to pick them up and wear them?  Stop it!”  Paul’s vulnerability here is powerful He reminds them how he first came to them — physically weak, probably with some obvious eye problem (we see hints of that later when he says he writes in “large letters” with his own hand), beaten up from previous persecution, maybe limping or scarred. He wasn’t impressive. He wasn’t polished. Yet they received him “as an angel of God, as Christ Jesus Himself.”  That memory makes his current grief even sharper. These people loved him when he was at his lowest. Now they’re drifting away from the very freedom he suffered to bring them.  This is classic Paul — he never separates his message from his life. He’s not just teaching “grace vs. law.” He’s living proof of it. He left the highest rung of the legalistic ladder because he met Christ, and he’s begging them not to climb back up that same ladder.  Ties straight into everything we’ve seen ◇You left slavery (to pagan gods or to the law) → don’t go back. ◇You received the Spirit by faith → don’t try to finish by works. ◇You’re sons crying “Abba, Father” → don’t return to orphan/slave living. ◇I became like you → you become like me (free).  His battered body becomes part of the argument:  This is what a life poured out for the true gospel looks like.  It’s not glamorous.  It’s not impressive by human standards.  But it’s free.  This passage always makes me appreciate how relational Paul is. Even in the middle of white-hot correction, he’s saying, “Remember how it was when I was with you? Let’s go back to that — not to the law, but to the love and freedom we shared.”  〰️GALATIANS 4〰️ AMP 17 These men [the Judaizers] eagerly seek you [to entrap you with honeyed words and attention, to win you over to their philosophy], not honorably [for their purpose is not honorable or worthy of consideration]. They want to isolate you [from us who oppose them] so that you will seek them  ME➡️ The motivation is pride-the control aspect (Like we discussed before - they want control like the greyhound wants the rabbit.  They don't even know what they want to do with it!) ….Or it's possibly something even darker.  〰️GALATIANS 〰️ 〰️WALK⛓️‍💥 FREE〰️ 〰️GOD HATES LEGALISM〰️ #biblestudygalatians #renegadebiblestudywithgrok #renegadebiblestudygalatians
